mission.title: Install and Configure Cozystack on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cozystack is a free and open-source PaaS framework that simplifies the management of virtual machines, Kubernetes clusters, and databases. Installing Cozystack allows you to build your own cloud infrastructure with ease and provides a cost-effective development environment.

steps.title: Add the Cozystack Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Cozystack Helm repository to your local Helm setup with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add cozystack https://charts.cozystack.io
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Cozystack using Helm
steps.description:
Install Cozystack in a new namespace called 'cozystack' with the specified version:
```bash
helm install cozystack cozystack/cozystack --version v1.0.2 --namespace cozystack --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Cozystack pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cozystack
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the Cozystack deployment to set resource limits:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cozystack --namespace cozystack
```
Then add the following under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    memory: "512Mi"
    cpu: "500m"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward the Cozystack service port to your local machine:
```bash
kubectl port-forward service/cozystack --namespace cozystack 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Cozystack release from your cluster:
```bash
helm uninstall cozystack --namespace cozystack
```

uninstall.title: Delete the Cozystack namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Cozystack namespace to clean up all associated resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your current configuration and data. You can use the following command to export your current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cozystack -o yaml > cozystack-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the Cozystack Helm chart:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cozystack
upgrade.description:
Upgrade your Cozystack installation to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade cozystack cozystack/cozystack --version v1.0.2 --namespace cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure they are running correctly after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cozystack
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for resource availability:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cozystack
```
Make sure your cluster has enough resources (CPU/memory) available.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Cozystack service, verify the service status:
```bash
kubectl get services --namespace cozystack
```
Ensure the service is correctly configured and the port forwarding is active.

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the logs of the pod:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cozystack
```
This will provide insight into what might be causing the crash.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cozystack will have all pods running in the 'cozystack' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the forwarded port. Resource limits should be configured as per your requirements.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cozystack https://charts.cozystack.io
helm repo update
helm install cozystack cozystack/cozystack --version v1.0.2 --namespace cozystack --create-namespace

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
